one_time_description: Treaty loan forgiveness - $6,295,884 in comprehensive land claim negotiation debt forgiven by Federal Government effective March 31, 2020
economic_notes: Nation operates logging, silviculture, retail store and lodge, fuel distribution, camp services, and economic development corporations. Multiple entities ceased or reduced operations subsequent to year end including Besikoh Fuel LP, Blackwater Camp Services Ltd., and Nazko Logging LP. COVID-19 impacted operations.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED for two reasons: (1) no approved budget presented as required by PSAS, and (2) insufficient audit evidence for Nazko Logging Limited Partnership and Nazko Economic Development Corp. and its subsidiaries. Large one-time item: $6,295,884 treaty loan forgiveness drives the annual surplus. Multiple consolidated subsidiaries (11 fully consolidated entities including at 51% for Nazbec LP and Nazbec Wood Processing). One entity on modified equity basis (Ndazkhot'en Forest Management Ltd.). Several entities ceased or significantly reduced operations subsequent to year end. TCA net book value not extractable from text provided. Capital lease obligations of $474,340 exist (not included in long_term_debt figure above). Auditor firm name not clearly identified - listed as Chartered Professional Accountants in Prince George, BC. total_government_transfers includes ISC $2,621,397 + FNHA $1,126,886 + BC $92,130 + CMHC $30,300 + CCATEC $52,233 + FNESC $3,596 = $3,926,542 plus treaty fund restricted cash contributions; osr calculated as total revenue minus identified government transfers.
